title: Education-only / no-medical-advice policy + refusal & advice-language checklist
context: Patient-Advocate-Research-Primer is an educational primer for cancer patient advocates, and must never drift into medical advice. This is the most critical guardrail for the project: all patient-facing content is high risk-tier and must carry a persistent 'not medical advice' frame. Before any patient-facing module is authored, a formal policy must define the hard boundary, enumerate prohibited output types, and provide a reusable advice-language checklist that reviewers and a future linter can apply.
objective: Define and publish the education-only / no-medical-advice policy, including the hard boundary between teaching how to read research and giving medical advice, an enumeration of prohibited output types, a reusable advice-language checklist, and the dual high-tier sign-off rule for all patient-facing content.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines the hard boundary: the project teaches how to read research in general; it never tells anyone what a finding means for their own or a loved one's cancer (no diagnosis, prognosis, treatment recommendation, trial-enrolment recommendation, or individualised guidance).

Enumerates prohibited output types: diagnosis, prognosis, treatment or trial recommendation, reassurance about outcomes, and any second-person prescriptive guidance ('you should', 'the best treatment for you').

Provides an advice-language checklist covering prescriptive second-person forms, superlative treatment claims ('best', 'only', 'proven'), reassurance/false-certainty patterns, and surrogate-as-cure framing — suitable for use by human reviewers and as the basis for a future linter.
Mandates the persistent 'not medical advice / education only' frame on all patient-facing content.
Mandates dual high-tier sign-off (credentialed oncologist + experienced patient advocate) for all patient-facing content before publication, with sign-offs recorded in the review ledger.
Specifies the role of the education-only assertion linter (task 004 follow-on) and how the checklist feeds it.
Is signed off by a credentialed oncologist (recorded in the review ledger).
Is signed off by an experienced patient advocate (recorded in the review ledger).
resources: PLAN.md (§ Goals and non-goals; § Data, licensing & compliance; § Quality, review & risk gates)
TASKS.md (Milestone M0 acceptance criteria for policy-002)
output: A policy document (committed to the repo) defining the education-only boundary, prohibited output enumeration, advice-language checklist, 'not medical advice' framing mandate, and dual high-tier sign-off rule — the authoritative reference for all content authors and reviewers.
